incorporations.original: It should be added that he was, in effect, paid to listen to
incorporations.replacement: Let it be added that he was paid to listen to
incorporations.reason: Gemini's 'Let it be added' renders Italian 'Aggiungasi' (a formal impersonal imperative) more faithfully than Claude's 'It should be added'; dropping 'in effect' tightens the sentence
Italian 'annunci' means advertisements/announcements; Gemini's 'advertisements' is the standard modern English equivalent and more precise than Claude's 'notices'
Italian 'ventri compressi dalla cintola' — 'ventri' means bellies, not waists; Gemini correctly identified the body part. 'Pinched' was substituted for 'compressed' to sharpen the image

incorporations.original: the generating flame of divine conceptions
incorporations.replacement: the generative flame of divine conceptions
incorporations.reason: Italian 'fiamma genitrice' — 'genitrice' is adjectival (generative/generatrix); Gemini's 'generative' is the correct English adjective form, where Claude's 'generating' reads as a participle

incorporations.original: full liberty of flight to the birds of prey chained in their native gaols
incorporations.replacement: to the birds of prey chained in the prisons of their homelands full freedom of flight
incorporations.reason: Gemini's word order ('granted to the birds... full freedom of flight') better mirrors the Italian sentence structure; 'freedom' is more natural in the collocation 'freedom of flight' than 'liberty'

incorporations.original: a swaggering inciter who, backed by his associates
incorporations.replacement: a swaggering instigator who, backed by his confederates
incorporations.reason: Italian 'aizzatore' = instigator/agitator; Gemini's 'instigator' is more standard than Claude's 'inciter'. 'Confederates' was chosen independently for its period colouring
Italian 'mandar tutto a soqquadro'; 'upside down' is the more natural and less quaint collocation; 'topsy-turvy' risks sounding arch

Italian 'raggi concentrati' literally = concentrated rays; 'sunshine' (closer to Gemini's 'sunbeams' family) captures the warmth metaphor better as a mass noun paralleling 'the sun of Italy' in the next clause

edgren_influences.note: Claude rendered 'ha il suo debito posto' as 'has its proper place'; Gemini as 'has its due place'. Edgren's primary gloss 'DUE' plus the secondary sense of DUTY/obligation prompted the more dynamic 'claims its rightful place', foregrounding the sense that this page is owed its position — not merely occupying it

Claude rendered 'A parte le proporzioni dell'ingegno' loosely as 'Setting aside all differences of genius'. Edgren confirms 'ingegno' = 'natural talent (genius)', and with 'proporzione' (proportion/scale) this licensed the more precise 'disproportion of their genius', preserving the author's point about unequal scale rather than mere difference

edgren_influences.edgren_definition: feel (perceive, be sensible of); smell; taste; judge (esteem); have
edgren_influences.english_choice: carries the soul of Giuseppe Mazzini
edgren_influences.note: Italian 'sente l'anima di Giuseppe Mazzini' — Claude chose 'breathes', Gemini 'bears'. Edgren's range (feel, perceive, sense, have) showed that 'sente' here means something closer to 'holds within itself' or 'conveys'. 'Carries' was chosen as more neutral and accurate than 'breathes' (too physiological) or 'bears' (too heavy)

edgren_influences.edgren_definition: (far capolino) peep in, peep through — diminutive of capo (head)
edgren_influences.note: Both drafts rendered 'fa capolino' as simply 'peeps in/through'. The Edgren context (diminutive of 'capo' = head) reinforced that 'far capolino' specifically evokes poking one's head through an opening, prompting the addition of 'through the doorway' to recover the spatial concreteness lost in a bare 'peeps in'
